page: The OVHcloud c2-120 is a compute-optimized server instance built on the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by a dedicated Intel Core Processor (Haswell, no TSX) running at 3.0 GHz. The configuration delivers 32 physical cores and 32 vCPUs, meaning there is exactly one thread per core with no hyperthreading. It is equipped with 120.0 GB of RAM, providing 3.75 GB of memory per core. Local storage consists of a 400 GB SSD, and network connectivity is supported by a 10 Gbps baseline bandwidth.

Performance benchmarks place this server in the average to strong tiers depending on the workload. It demonstrates strong performance in cached small-block memory read bandwidth at 3545.25 GB/sec and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression at 253.18 GB/sec. Passmark CPU extended instructions also score in the top 25% at 48100.6. However, its LLM inference capabilities are limited, with text generation on a 7B model registering at a weak 6.29 tokens/sec and a 135M model at a poor 37.21 tokens/sec. General CPU benchmarks, such as Geekbench multi-core (11504.0) and Passmark CPU (40620.12), remain in the average range.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the c2-120 offers a dense allocation of 32 dedicated physical cores without hyperthreading, making it suitable for workloads requiring predictable, non-shared CPU execution. The lack of hyperthreading and AVX-512 support represents a tradeoff for highly parallelized vector-heavy tasks, but the 10 Gbps network baseline and 400 GB local SSD provide balanced support for data-intensive operations. Appropriate workloads include multi-threaded data decompression, software compilation, static web serving, and general-purpose compute tasks that do not require GPU acceleration.
description: The OVHcloud c2-120 is a compute-optimized server featuring 32 dedicated physical cores and 32 vCPUs on an x86_64 Intel Haswell architecture running at 3.0 GHz. It is configured with 120.0 GB of RAM, a 400 GB local SSD, and a 10 Gbps baseline network interface. Operating on the KVM hypervisor, this instance provides predictable CPU performance with no hyperthreading. Benchmarks indicate strong capabilities in cached small-block memory reads and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, alongside average general CPU performance. While it lacks GPU acceleration and exhibits weak text generation speeds for large language models, its dedicated core allocation makes it highly suitable for software compilation, static web serving, and multi-threaded data processing workloads.
og_description: OVHcloud c2-120 is a compute-optimized server with 32 dedicated Intel cores, 120 GB RAM, and a 400 GB SSD. It offers 10 Gbps network bandwidth and excels in multi-threaded decompression and cached memory reads.
meta_description: OVHcloud c2-120 is a compute-optimized KVM server featuring 32 dedicated Intel cores, 120 GB RAM, and a 400 GB SSD with 10 Gbps network speed.
tagline: A dedicated 32-core compute-optimized server featuring local SSD storage and high-speed cached memory performance for parallel processing workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated Intel physical cores with no hyperthreading
120 GB of system memory and 400 GB local SSD storage
10 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with 1 public IPv4 address
Strong multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and cached memory read speeds
Suited for software compilation, static web serving, and parallel data processing
